procedures: 1. Investigate for prominent physical damage to any components before proceeding. This may include wires with damaged insulation, dents or large cracks visible on components, or missing wires 2. Verify the LTV is not stuck by confirming the wheels are not wedged in divots, rocks, and are otherwise intact. 3. Locate the power distribution and diagnostic (PDD) component by identifying an irregular-shaped white housing with four power bus connections 4. On the PDD display, verify the vehicle is in recovery mode and not some other error state, this is indicated by the error code 4800 and a red light by the recovery switch, labeled “RECO” 5. If a different error is displayed, that error must be fixed prior to exiting recovery mode 6. Via the Power Override Panel for Subsystems, labeled “POPS”, move switches for “MPS”, “GNC”, and “CDH” to the “OFF” position 6. Locate and press the Software Fault Acknowledge button, labeled “SFA” 7. Move the Power Optimization Mode switch, labeled “POM”, to the “OFF” position, verify change by confirming a blue indicator light 8. Open the protective cover for the External Battery Disconnect switch, labeled “EBD” 9. Press and hold the Computer Software Reboot button, labeled “CSR” 10. While holding the CSR button, move the EBD switch to the “ON” position 11. Release the CSR button 12. Wait 5-10 seconds for the reboot to take effect. Successful reboot will be indicated by the green light on the software management box turning on. 13. Move the EBD switch to the “OFF” position and close the cover 14. You should now see a red blinking status light appear on the software management box. 15. Via POPS, move the switches for MPS, GNC, and CDH to the “ON” position 16. Open the protective cover for the CTRL switch on the software management box. 17. On the software management box, hold down the black ALT button while flipping the CTRL switch on which will also turn the red status light off. 18. Release the ALT button 19. To test the software reboot, hold down both the test switch on the software management box and the CSR button until the green status light blinks. If the green status light is not blinking after 10 seconds, announce green light not blinking. 20. Hold down the black ALT button while switching off the control switch. The green status light should now be on to signify successful reboot. 21. Close the protective cover of the CTRL switch. 22. Open the protective cover for the recovery switch, labeled “RECO” 23. Move the RECO switch to the “OFF” position and close the cover 24. Verify the LTV has exited recovery mode by confirming the error code 4800 no longer displays 25. Announce ERM success

error_procedures.description: NAV Restart & Manual Return to Home
procedures: 1. Locate the NAV Control Panel by identifying a large white rectangular component with a green light in the top-left corner 2. Flip the primary control switch under the green light to the far left, entering “HAND” mode. Verify mode switch by confirming the indicator light turns blue 3. Identify the “LIDAR RESET” button 4. Flush and restart the LIDAR system by depressing this button for 5 seconds. The LIDAR button indicator will light red upon depressing this button. The green indicator light on the software management box will turn on upon successful LIDAR system flush. 5. Identify the “NAV RESET” button 6. Flush and restart the NAV System by depressing this button for 5 seconds. The NAV button indicator will light red upon depressing this button. The red indicator light on the software management box will turn on upon successful NAV system flush. 7. Enact the reset by moving the primary control switch to the “OFF” position, wait for 10 seconds 8. Move the primary control switch to “HAND” mode. Verify mode switch by confirming the indicator light is blue 9. Identify the autonomous systems indicators toggle switch labeled “ASITS” 10. Move the ASITS switch to the “ON” position, verify mode switch by confirming the indicator light is yellow and the remaining indicators are on 11. Identify the autonomous navigation system lock labeled “ANAV BLOCK” 12. Move this switch to the “ON” position, verify mode switch by confirming the indicator light is blue 13. Identify the manual return to home switch labeled “ANAV RTH” 14. Move this switch to the “ON” position, verify mode switch by confirming the indicator light is blue 15. Identify the autonomy confidence adjustment dial labeled “ACA” 16. Rotate this dial counterclockwise to manually reduce the confidence scale of the autonomous navigation algorithm 17. Move the “ANAV BLOCK” switch to the “OFF” position 18. Move the “ASITS” switch to the “OFF” position 19. Identify the autonomous communication algorithm switch, labeled “COMM” 20. Turn the switch to the “SEC” position, and verify mode switch by confirming the indicator light is blue and the status buttons on the software management box have turned off. 21. Move the primary control switch to the “AUTO” position, verify mode switch by confirming indicator light is green 22. Announce NAV Restart & Manual Return to Home steps complete

1. Locate the fuse box by identifying a large gray rectangular housing  2. Lift the lid of the enclosure, exposing a black protective barrier, ensure the lid locks in the upright position 3. Remove the fuse disconnect by pulling the exposed black handle located towards the top of the box, while holding the enclosure steady 4. Remove the protective barrier by pulling outward from the top, while holding the enclosure steady 5. Use the provided fuse-puller tool to safely remove both fuses and properly discard them 6. Locate the replacement fuses 7. Using the fuse-puller tool, place a new fuse in the grips of the tool 8. Insert the fuse into the empty slot, moderate force may be required 9. Repeat steps 7 and 8 for the other fuse 10. Replace the black protective barrier 11. Reinsert the fuse disconnect in the proper orientation 12. Close the lid of the enclosure by lifting slightly outwards and pulling down 13. Announce Backup Fuse Error steps complete

1. Locate the antenna communications control component by identifying a square gray box with white turn knobs and a strip of indicator lights 2. Locate the phase control knob, labeled “PHS”, rotate this until maximum possible RSSI improvement is achieved 3. Locate the modulation control knob, labeled “MOD”, rotate until maximum possible RSSI improvement is achieved 4. Locate the amplitude control knob, labeled “AMP”, rotate until maximum possible RSSI improvement is achieved 5. If the green indicator has not been lit, you may rotate these controls until maximum RSSI strength has been achieved, this is indicated by the green indicator being lit 6. Now, you should see a red blinking status light appear on the white rectangular box, the software management box 7. Flip open the cover of the CTRL switch 8. On the software management box, hold down the black ALT button while flipping the control switch on which will also turn the red status light off 9. To finish resetting the software for the RSSI component, press the black ALT button and the red RESET button at the same time. You should see the red and green status lights both turn on upon successful reset 10. To test the software reboot, hold down the test switch on the software management box until the green status light blinks. If the green status light is not blinking after 20 seconds, announce green light not blinking 11. Hold down the black ALT button while switching off the CTRL switch. Close the cover on the CTRL switch. The green status light should now be on to signify successful reboot 12. Announce Poor Comms RSSI steps complete

1. Locate the power distribution and diagnostic (PDD) component by identifying an irregular-shaped white housing with four power bus connections 2. Via the Power Override Panel for Subsystems, labeled “POPS”, move the Command and Data Handling switch, labeled “CDH” to the “OFF” position 3. Wait 5 seconds 4. Move the CDH switch to the “ON” position 5. Locate the antenna communications control component by identifying a square gray box with white turn knobs and a strip of indicator lights 6. If maximum RSSI strength is not indicated, proceed with the following steps 7. Locate the phase control knob, labeled “PHS”, rotate this until maximum possible RSSI improvement is achieved 8. Locate the modulation control knob, labeled “MOD”, rotate until maximum possible RSSI improvement is achieved 9. Locate the amplitude control knob, labeled “AMP”, rotate until maximum possible RSSI improvement is achieved 10. If the green indicator has not been lit, you may rotate these controls until maximum RSSI strength has been achieved, this is indicated by the green indicator being lit 11. Now, you should see a red blinking status light appear on the white rectangular box, the software management box 12. On the software management box, hold down the black ALT button while flipping the control switch on which will also turn the red status light off 13. To finish resetting the software for the RSSI component, press the black ALT button and the red RESET button at the same time. You should see the red and green status lights both turn on upon successful reset 14. To test the software reboot, hold down the test switch on the software management box until the green status light blinks. If the green status light is not blinking after 10 seconds, announce green light not blinking 15. Hold down the black ALT button while switching off the control switch. The green status light should now be on to signify successful reboot 16. Announce Comms Reboot steps complete

1. Locate the power distribution and diagnostic (PDD) component by identifying an irregular-shaped white housing with four power bus connections 2. Locate the Power Override Panel for Subsystems, labeled “POPS” 3. Via POPS, move switches for “MPS”, “GNC”, “VSI”, and “CDH” to the “OFF” position 4. Check for any loose bus cables 5. If a cable is deemed loose, fully remove the cable from its socket 6. Firmly reinsert the cable into its socket 7. If no cable is deemed loose, fully remove and reinsert each cable into the respective sockets 8. Via POPS, move switches for “MPS”, “GNC”, “VSI”, and “CDH” to the “ON” position 9. Wait 5-10 seconds to verify the error clears before proceeding 10. Announce Subsystem Power Bus steps complete

1. Locate the dust sensor by identifying a cylindrical white housing smaller than the palm of a hand 2. Rotate the sensor body counterclockwise to unscrew it from its base and stow properly 3. Locate the replacement sensor and twist the protective cap counterclockwise to remove it, you may put the protective cap on the broken dust sensor 4. Insert the replacement sensor into the base and rotate clockwise until firmly secured 5. Announce Dust Sensor steps complete

1. Locate the Scientific Management box by identifying a small gray box with a single protruding indicator light 2. Verify this indicator light is illuminated green, an unlit indicator implies an upstream power distribution issue 3. Locate the Scientific Power Distribution Module by identifying a small black housing with electrical terminals and six indicators 4. Take note of any indicators that are illuminated and which side they appear on 5. Locate the power distribution and diagnostic (PDD) component by identifying an irregular-shaped white housing with four power bus connections 6. Via the Power Override Panel for Subsystems, labeled “POPS”, move the Mission Payload Subsystem switch, labeled “MPS” to the “OFF” position 7. To prevent damage from unexpected electrical surges during operation, remove the power bus from its socket before proceeding 8. Corresponding to the side that had a lit indicator, locate the fuse cartridge for that side and remove it from the component 9. Prepare and insert a replacement fuse cartridge into the empty section 10. Via POPS, reinsert the power bus 11. Move the MPS switch to the “ON” position 12. Verify the scientific management indicator is illuminated green and no indicators are illuminated on the power distribution module 13. Announce Small Fuse Box steps complete
